comments.body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The PR introduces 7 new stock records with comprehensive financial analytics and screening metadata. It simultaneously refactors the codebase by extracting financial calculations, screener logic, ranking rules, and volatility computations into new dedicated modules. Build configuration and linting rules are updated to support enhanced tooling and test infrastructure. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Stock Records**
`data/stock-records/AVAV.json`, `data/stock-records/COIN.json`, `data/stock-records/HOOD.json`, `data/stock-records/KTOS.json`, `data/stock-records/LEU.json`, `data/stock-records/RTX.json`, `data/stock-records/TSLA.json`|Seven new JSON stock record files added with structured financial data including classification, confidence scoring, CAGR models, valuations, consensus targets, screener results with reality checks, QCS scores, and lastUpdated timestamps.| |**Screener Engine**
`src/lib/domain/screener/types.ts`, `src/lib/domain/screener/engine.ts`|New screener domain module with TypeScript types for Yahoo data, earnings trends, valuation inputs, reality checks, and a comprehensive `computeScreener` function that routes growth scoring, applies risk multipliers, and validates via stabilization/revision/earnings-surprise checks.| |**Ranking & Deployment Logic**
`src/lib/domain/ranking/dashboard.ts`, `src/lib/domain/ranking/rules.ts`, `src/lib/domain/ranking/momentum.ts`, `src/lib/domain/ranking/quality.ts`|Four new ranking modules extracted from monolithic deployment logic: dashboard builds ranked stock lists with deployment decisions and weighted scoring; rules define deployment thresholds and value-floor eligibility; momentum computes Z-score bonuses from 6m/1m return differentials; quality calculates ROE and FCF-yield bonuses.| |**Volatility Logic**
`src/lib/domain/volatility/logic.ts`|New module implementing composite world volatility signal computation with weighted averaging of VIX/VSTOXX components, staleness filtering, and classification into action/band/tone categories.| |**Update Scripts**
`scripts/lib/update/apply.ts`, `scripts/lib/update/fs.ts`, `scripts/lib/update/qcs.ts`|New update utility modules: `applyUpdates` populates stock fields from Yahoo data and runs screener/QCS; `atomicWriteJson` safely writes JSON via temp file rename; `computeQCS` calculates earnings/flow/revisions scores.| |**Finance Core**
`src/lib/domain/finance/core.ts`, `lib/finance-core.d.ts`|Updated finance module with TypeScript types for `parsePercent`, `parseDisplayPrice`, and forward-return functions; removed `.d.ts` declaration file (migration to implementation-only export).| |**Path Infrastructure**
`src/lib/server/infrastructure/paths.ts`, `lib/project-paths.js`, `lib/project-paths.d.ts`, `src/lib/server/findings.ts`|Relocated path constants from `lib/project-paths` to new server infrastructure module; updated `findings.ts` import reference.| |**Deployment Refactoring**
`src/lib/domain/deployment.ts`|Converted to re-export module delegating all logic to four new ranking submodules, reducing file from 388 to 4 lines.| |**World Volatility Updates**
`src/lib/server/world-vol.ts`, `src/lib/server/infrastructure/yahoo.ts`|Simplified volatility signal fetching by delegating to imported domain logic; extracted Yahoo client config to dedicated infrastructure module with validation/notice suppression.| |**Script Refactoring**
`scripts/update-data.ts`, `scripts/lib/screener.ts`, `scripts/one-off/evaluate_methodology.ts`|Refactored main update script to delegate work to new modules; simplified screener branch detection; consolidated array/string operations in evaluation script.| |**Build Configuration**
`eslint.config.js`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.scripts.json`|Updated ESLint to use `sonarjs` plugin with simplified rules; added new npm scripts (`prepare`, `check:watch`, `lint:all`, `add-new-stocks`) and dev dependencies; expanded TypeScript exclusions for `scripts/sandbox/**`.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Script as scripts/update-data.ts participant YF as yahooFinance participant Apply as applyUpdates participant Screener as computeScreener participant QCS as computeQCS participant Write as atomicWriteJson Script->>YF: Fetch quotes & summaries YF-->>Script: quote, summary, historicalData Script->>Apply: Apply updates to stock Apply->>Screener: Compute screener Screener-->>Apply: screener result Apply->>QCS: Compute QCS QCS-->>Apply: qcs scores Apply-->>Script: Updated stock Script->>Write: Persist to disk Write-->>Script: File written ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~65 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - **#7**: Modifies `src/lib/server/world-vol.ts` with freshness/fallback logic updates for volatility component handling, related to the new volatility logic module extraction. ## Poem > 🐰 *Hop! Hop! The refactor dance,* > *New modules spring in structured prance,* > *Seven stocks in records neat,* > *Screener rules and ranks complete,* > *Codebase flowers, clean and bright!* 🌱
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 41.86% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title 'feat: expand stock universe and rebase' accurately reflects the main changes: adding new stock records (AVAV, RTX, KTOS, TSLA, COIN, HOOD, LEU) and rebasing develop onto main with conflict resolution. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `develop`

## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request primarily focuses on expanding the system's stock coverage by integrating new companies and significantly enhancing the underlying financial data processing and analysis infrastructure. It also includes a major refactoring of core financial logic into a more modular and maintainable structure, alongside updates to development tooling to improve code quality and streamline workflows. ### Highlights * **Expanded Stock Universe**: Added new stock records for AeroVironment (AVAV), RTX Corporation (RTX), Kratos Defense (KTOS), Tesla (TSLA), Coinbase (COIN), and Robinhood (HOOD), significantly broadening the coverage of the stock analysis system. * **Core Financial Logic Refactoring**: Restructured and refactored the core financial calculation and data update logic. Functions previously in `lib/finance-core.js` and `scripts/update-data.ts` have been moved into a new, modular `src/lib/domain` structure, enhancing maintainability and clarity. This includes new modules for ranking, screener engine, volatility logic, and data application. * **ESLint Configuration Update**: Updated the ESLint configuration to include `eslint-plugin-sonarjs` for improved code quality analysis. Several ESLint rules were adjusted, and file exclusions were refined, indicating a stronger focus on code quality and consistency across the project. * **Build System and Script Enhancements**: Introduced `lint-staged` for pre-commit hooks to enforce code style and quality. Added new scripts for `add-new-stocks` and improved existing ones, streamlining development and data management workflows. Several sandbox scripts were also reorganized into a dedicated directory. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a significant refactoring of the codebase, moving core financial calculation, screener, and deployment ranking logic into a new `src/lib/domain` directory for improved modularity. This includes the creation of new files for dashboard ranking, momentum, quality, and rules, as well as a new `screener/types.ts` file. The `update-data.ts` script has been refactored to utilize these new modular functions. Additionally, the ESLint configuration has been updated to include SonarJS rules and simplify existing configurations. Several stock data files have also been updated. There are two critical issues identified in the `src/lib/domain/ranking/rules.ts` file: the `deploymentForPass` function uses undefined variables `basePass` and `stabPass`, and the `ETF_HURDLE_RETURN` constant is declared twice.

## Pull request overview This PR rebases develop onto main while expanding the tracked stock universe and refactoring several “domain” concerns (volatility signal, screener, and deployment/ranking) into more modular TypeScript code, plus updating the Yahoo update pipeline. **Changes:** - Added/updated stock-record JSONs for AVAV, RTX, KTOS, TSLA, COIN, and HOOD (plus updated LEU record). - Refactored deployment/ranking and screener logic into `src/lib/domain/**` modules and re-exported via `src/lib/domain/deployment.ts`. - Introduced server-side infrastructure helpers (`yahooFinance` wrapper, project paths), and excluded `scripts/sandbox/**` from TS compilation. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 30 out of 44 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tsconfig.scripts.json | Excludes `scripts/sandbox/**` from script TS compilation. | | tsconfig.json | Excludes `scripts/sandbox/**` from repo TS compilation. | | src/lib/server/world-vol.ts | Switches to shared Yahoo client + moves composite-signal logic into domain module. | | src/lib/server/infrastructure/yahoo.ts | Adds shared configured YahooFinance client instance. | | src/lib/server/infrastructure/paths.ts | Adds centralized project path constants (data/stock-records). | | src/lib/server/findings.ts | Updates server findings loader to use new path helper. | | src/lib/domain/volatility/logic.ts | Extracts world-vol constants and composite-signal builder into domain layer. | | src/lib/domain/screener/types.ts | Adds typed structures for screener inputs/outputs and Yahoo summary fragments. | | src/lib/domain/screener/engine.ts | Adds new TS screener engine implementation with reality checks. | | src/lib/domain/ranking/rules.ts | Introduces deployment rules + value-floor heuristic (currently has compile issues). | | src/lib/domain/ranking/quality.ts | Adds quality bonus computation (ROE + FCF yield). | | src/lib/domain/ranking/momentum.ts | Adds cross-sectional momentum stats + per-stock momentum bonus. | | src/lib/domain/ranking/dashboard.ts | Adds dashboard builder + deployment rank calculation. | | src/lib/domain/finance/core.ts | Moves/updates finance parsing + forward-return helpers. | | src/lib/domain/deployment.ts | Re-exports ranking modules as the public deployment API. | | scripts/update-data.ts | Refactors update pipeline to use new `applyUpdates` + atomic write helper (has broken import). | | scripts/sandbox/test-yf.ts | Adds sandbox YahooFinance experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-yahoo.ts | Adds sandbox Yahoo chart experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-screener.ts | Adds sandbox screener experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-screener-export.ts | Adds sandbox screener-export experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-ranks.ts | Adds sandbox ranking experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-pe.ts | Adds sandbox deployment/PE experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-kkr2.ts | Adds sandbox screener experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/test-kkr.ts | Adds sandbox screener experiment script. | | scripts/sandbox/check-units.js | Adds sandbox unit/currency sanity check script. | | scripts/sandbox/check-nvo.ts | Adds sandbox check script for NVO classification. | | scripts/one-off/evaluate_methodology.ts | Refactors one-off methodology evaluation script formatting/imports. | | scripts/lib/update/qcs.ts | Extracts QCS computation into its own module. | | scripts/lib/update/fs.ts | Adds atomic JSON write helper. | | scripts/lib/update/apply.ts | Extracts Yahoo-to-record update logic into reusable function (has unused import). | | scripts/lib/screener.ts | Tweaks cyclical branch detection condition formatting. | | package.json | Adds dev tooling scripts and lint-staged config; adds several lint-related deps. | | lib/project-paths.js | Removes legacy project paths helper. | | lib/project-paths.d.ts | Removes legacy project paths type declaration. | | lib/finance-core.d.ts | Removes legacy finance-core type declaration. | | eslint.config.js | Adds sonarjs + adjusts TS/Unicorn rules; changes ignore list (needs sandbox ignore). | | data/stock-records/TSLA.json | Adds TSLA stock record. | | data/stock-records/RTX.json | Adds RTX stock record. | | data/stock-records/LEU.json | Updates LEU stock record. | | data/stock-records/KTOS.json | Adds KTOS stock record. | | data/stock-records/HOOD.json | Adds HOOD stock record. | | data/stock-records/COIN.json | Adds COIN stock record. | | data/stock-records/AVAV.json | Adds AVAV stock record. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2) **src/lib/domain/finance/core.ts:32** * `parseDisplayPrice`’s pence detection regex (`/\d\s*p$/i`) only matches values with a space before `p` (e.g. "150 p"), but common inputs like "150p" / "250.5p" won’t match and will be parsed 100x too large. Adjust the detection to also handle no-space suffixes (and consider trimming/normalizing first). **src/lib/domain/finance/core.ts:88** * `calcForwardReturn` no longer applies `dividendYieldPct`, but `ForwardReturnParams` still includes it and `calcForwardScenarios` still passes it through. This is misleading API surface area; either remove `dividendYieldPct` from the types/params, or re-introduce dividend yield into the return calculation (and update docs/tests accordingly).
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 20** > [!NOTE] > Due to the large number of review comments, Critical, Major severity comments were prioritized as inline comments. > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (3)
> >
> src/lib/server/world-vol.ts (1)
> > `224-237`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **This branch can publish a VIX-only signal and still skips the required fallback.** > > `buildCompositeWorldVolSignal()` in `src/lib/domain/volatility/logic.ts` (Lines 49-56) drops stale components, so the `vix.fresh` path can still return a classification when `vstoxx` is stale. If `vix` is stale, we go straight to `available: false` without attempting the URTH options fallback this module is supposed to use. > > Based on learnings, `src/lib/server/world-vol.ts`: World volatility signal classification: VIX/VSTOXX composite (70/30 weighted) with URTH options fallback; < 15 = buy leverage, 15-25 = hold, > 25 = sell/reduce. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@src/lib/server/world-vol.ts` around lines 224 - 237, The current branch can > return a VIX-only composite because buildCompositeWorldVolSignal drops stale > components; change the logic so you only return a composite signal when both vix > and vstoxx are fresh (e.g., require vix.fresh && vstoxx.fresh before > calling/returning buildCompositeWorldVolSignal), and if either component is > stale (especially when vix is not fresh) fall through to invoke the URTH options > fallback routine instead of returning available: false; reference the vix and > vstoxx variables, buildCompositeWorldVolSignal(), and the URTH options fallback > logic so the code attempts that fallback before emitting the unavailable > response. > ``` > >
> >
>
> eslint.config.js (2)
> > `29-30`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **Restore the repo's TS/TSX lint contract in this override.** > > The matcher at line 29 skips `.tsx` files, `explicit-function-return-type` is not enforced for `.ts` files, and `consistent-type-imports` at line 58 lacks the required `fixStyle: 'inline-type-imports'` configuration. Additionally, the `.svelte` override block (lines 74-79) does not include rules to relax `explicit-function-return-type` for Svelte files. New source files can pass lint while violating the repo standard. > >
> Suggested fix > > ```diff > - files: ['**/*.{ts,svelte}'], > + files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx,svelte}'], > @@ > - '@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports': ['error', { prefer: 'type-imports' }], > + '@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type': ['error', { allowExpressions: true }], > + '@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports': [ > + 'error', > + { prefer: 'type-imports', fixStyle: 'inline-type-imports' } > + ], > @@ > { > files: ['**/*.svelte'], > + rules: { > + '@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type': 'off' > + }, > languageOptions: { > parserOptions: { > parser: tseslint.parser > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@eslint.config.js` around lines 29 - 30, Restore TypeScript/TSX linting by > updating the override matcher to include .tsx (change files matcher from > '**/*.{ts,svelte}' to include tsx), re-enable the explicit-function-return-type > rule for .ts (ensure the rule is present in the TypeScript override where files > include .ts/.tsx), add the required option fixStyle: 'inline-type-imports' to > the consistent-type-imports rule configuration, and in the Svelte override (the > block that relaxes rules for .svelte files) explicitly relax/disable > explicit-function-return-type so Svelte components are exempt while the > repo-wide .ts/.tsx rule remains enforced. > ``` > >
> > --- > > `36-38`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **Node.js compatibility issue: `import.meta.dirname` requires v20.11.0 or later.** > > `package.json` allows `node: ">=20"`, which includes versions 20.0.0 through 20.10.x where `import.meta.dirname` does not exist. ESLint will crash with a ReferenceError on those supported runtimes. Use `fileURLToPath(new URL('.', import.meta.url))` instead, or bump the minimum Node version to 20.11.0. > >
> Portable fix > > ```diff > +import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'; > import js from '@eslint/js'; > import prettierConfig from 'eslint-config-prettier'; > import svelte from 'eslint-plugin-svelte'; > import unicorn from 'eslint-plugin-unicorn'; > import globals from 'globals'; > import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint'; > import sonarjs from 'eslint-plugin-sonarjs'; > > +const tsconfigRootDir = fileURLToPath(new URL('.', import.meta.url)); > + > export default tseslint.config( > { > ignores: [ > @@ > parserOptions: { > projectService: true, > - tsconfigRootDir: import.meta.dirname, > + tsconfigRootDir, > extraFileExtensions: ['.svelte'] > } > }, > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@eslint.config.js` around lines 36 - 38, Replace the non-portable > import.meta.dirname usage in the eslint config: in the parserOptions block > (tsconfigRootDir currently set to import.meta.dirname) use a file URL-to-path > expression instead to support Node versions <20.11.0 — import fileURLToPath from > 'url' and compute tsconfigRootDir from fileURLToPath(new URL('.', > import.meta.url)); alternatively, if you prefer, update package.json to require > Node >=20.11.0 so import.meta.dirname is guaranteed available. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🟡 Minor comments (6)
scripts/one-off/evaluate_methodology.ts-70-71 (1)
`70-71`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Use `Number.parseFloat` to comply with the unicorn linting rule and maintain consistency with the rest of the codebase.** The project's ESLint configuration enforces the unicorn plugin's recommended rules, which require `Number.parseFloat` instead of `parseFloat`. All other files in the codebase (src/lib/**, tests/, scripts/lib/) consistently use `Number.parseFloat`, making this file's usage of plain `parseFloat` an outlier.
Suggested fix ```diff - const price = typeof priceParam === 'string' ? parseFloat(String(priceParam).replaceAll(/[^0-9.]/g, '')) : priceParam; - const target = typeof targetParam === 'string' ? parseFloat(String(targetParam).replaceAll(/[^0-9.]/g, '')) : targetParam; + const price = typeof priceParam === 'string' ? Number.parseFloat(String(priceParam).replaceAll(/[^0-9.]/g, '')) : priceParam; + const target = typeof targetParam === 'string' ? Number.parseFloat(String(targetParam).replaceAll(/[^0-9.]/g, '')) : targetParam; ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@scripts/one-off/evaluate_methodology.ts` around lines 70 - 71, Replace the direct calls to parseFloat in the price/target parsing expressions with Number.parseFloat to satisfy the unicorn lint rule and maintain consistency; update the two expressions that reference priceParam and targetParam (the constants price and target) to use Number.parseFloat(String(...).replaceAll(/[^0-9.]/g, '')) when the param is a string, preserving the existing replaceAll regex and fallback to the original param when not a string. ```
src/lib/domain/screener/types.ts-123-124 (1)
`123-124`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Use integer literals for these thresholds.** `1.0` violates `unicorn/no-zero-fractions` from the ESLint configuration.
Proposed fix ```diff - fPERG: 1.0, - tPERG: 1.0, + fPERG: 1, + tPERG: 1, ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/lib/domain/screener/types.ts` around lines 123 - 124, The numeric thresholds fPERG and tPERG are using float literals (1.0) which triggers the unicorn/no-zero-fractions rule; change their values to integer literals (1) in the definitions for fPERG and tPERG in types.ts so they use 1 instead of 1.0 to satisfy the linter. ```
src/lib/server/infrastructure/paths.ts-1-8 (1)
`1-8`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Switch `node:path` import to default style to satisfy unicorn/import-style.** This violates `unicorn/import-style`, which requires default import for the `node:path` module.
Proposed fix ```diff -import { resolve } from 'node:path'; +import path from 'node:path'; @@ -export const PROJECT_ROOT = resolve(process.cwd()); -export const DATA_DIR = resolve(PROJECT_ROOT, 'data'); -export const STOCK_RECORDS_DIR = resolve(DATA_DIR, 'stock-records'); +export const PROJECT_ROOT = path.resolve(process.cwd()); +export const DATA_DIR = path.resolve(PROJECT_ROOT, 'data'); +export const STOCK_RECORDS_DIR = path.resolve(DATA_DIR, 'stock-records'); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/lib/server/infrastructure/paths.ts` around lines 1 - 8, The import of resolve from 'node:path' violates unicorn/import-style; change the import to a default import (e.g., import path from 'node:path') and update all uses of resolve to path.resolve so PROJECT_ROOT, DATA_DIR, and STOCK_RECORDS_DIR keep working (ensure you update the import statement and replace calls to resolve(...) with path.resolve(...)). ```
src/lib/domain/finance/core.ts-14-24 (1)
`14-24`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Fix lint violations in parsing helpers.** Line 14 triggers `prefer-regexp-exec`: use `RegExp#exec()` instead of `String#match()`. Line 24 triggers `unicorn/prefer-string-replace-all`: use `String#replaceAll()` instead of `String#replace()` for global replacements.
